title: Comparing does not work if a JSON column field have different dynamicType
description: ### Company or project name _No response_ ### Describe the unexpected behaviour **Example 1:** We have a JSON field in ClickHouse that sometimes contains datetime values and sometimes strings. When we use this field in a WHERE clause, ClickHouse automatically tries to parse the literal in the condition as a DateTime and if it is not parseable, it throws CANNOT_PARSE_DATETIME exception. **Example 2:** Another weird behaviour is when a JSON field has both String and Int64 values. It returns Int64 and String rows if where conditions literal is String but throws NO_COMMON_TYPE exception if where condition is integer. See below screenshot ### Which ClickHouse versions are affected? it was working in the previous versions (24.9.2.42) as you can see in the screenshot below. We upgraded to newer version(25.4.1) since JSON column is not prod-ready and we started to encounter this error. ### How to reproduce ``` CREATE TABLE events ( ts DateTime, attributes JSON ) ENGINE = MergeTree ORDER BY (ts); INSERT INTO events FORMAT JSONEachRow {"ts":"2025-09-18 12:00:00","attributes":{"label":"order"}} INSERT INTO events FORMAT JSONEachRow {"ts":"2023-09-18 12:00:00","attributes":{"label":"2024-09-18 12:00:00"}} SELECT * FROM events where attributes.label = 'xyz'; INSERT INTO events FORMAT JSONEachRow {"ts":"2025-09-18 12:00:00","attributes":{"label_1":"5"}} INSERT INTO events FORMAT JSONEachRow {"ts":"2023-09-18 12:00:00","attributes":{"label_1": 5}} SELECT attributes.label_1, dynamicType(attributes.label_1) FROM events; SELECT * FROM events where attributes.label_1 = '5'; ``` ### Expected behavior Does not return rows if literal type in where condition and column type are not the same. It was working in this way. ### Error message and/or stacktrace ``` Received exception from server (version 25.8.4): Code: 41. DB::Exception: Received from localhost:9000. DB::Exception: Cannot parse datetime: while converting 'xyz' to DateTime: while executing 'FUNCTION equals(attributes.label : 0, 'xyz'_String :: 1) -> equals(__table1.attributes.label, 'xyz'_String) Nullable(UInt8) : 2': While executing MergeTreeSelect(pool: ReadPool, algorithm: Thread). (CANNOT_PARSE_DATETIME) ``` OR ``` Received exception from server (version 25.8.4): Code: 386. DB::Exception: Received from localhost:9000. DB::Exception: There is no supertype for types String, UInt8 because some of them are String/FixedString/Enum and some of them are not: while executing 'FUNCTION equals(attributes.label_1 : 0, 5_UInt8 :: 1) -> equals(__table1.attributes.label_1, 5_UInt8) Nullable(UInt8) : 2': While executing MergeTreeSelect(pool: ReadPool, algorithm: Thread). (NO_COMMON_TYPE) ``` ### Additional context _No response_
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE events_v2 (ts TIMESTAMP, attributes JSON) PARTITION BY RANGE (to_char(ts, 'YYYYMMDD')) SUBPARTITION BY HASH (ts);
SELECT JSON_VALUE(attributes, '$.label'), CASE WHEN JSON_TYPE(attributes, '$.label') = 'NUMBER' THEN 'NUMBER' ELSE 'STRING' END AS type FROM events_v2 WHERE JSON_VALUE(attributes, '$.label') = '5';
key_bug_features: JSON column with mixed types causes comparison errors
